title: Per-file escaped diff fragments + full cockpit sections
intent_summary: Adds the File Walkthrough substrate (one independently-escaped diff fragment per changed file) and the agent-authored analytical sections — Executive Summary, Review Route, Behavior Changes, Risk Map, File Walkthrough, Suspicious Omissions, and a Test Checklist driven by read-only runner detection.

review_route.reason: Start here: the schema and validator define the shape every other section must honour.
The per-file fragment id and index helpers — the substrate the walkthrough renders.
Wires per-file diffs into the collection step; check the fragments dir is rebuilt cleanly.
Verify detection is genuinely read-only before trusting the Test Checklist.

behavior_changes.summary: The collector now emits one escaped diff fragment per changed file plus an ordered index.
behavior_changes.detail: fragments/<id>.html and fragments.json are written alongside the preserved whole-diff fragment.

behavior_changes.summary: Non-ASCII paths now round-trip as raw UTF-8 (core.quotePath disabled).
behavior_changes.detail: Previously git C-quoted them, so the stored path no longer matched the file on disk.

risk_map.reason: Untrusted file paths are turned into on-disk fragment filenames; a naive mapping could allow path traversal or collisions.
challenge_questions: Can any path produce a fragment filename that escapes the fragments/ directory?
Do two distinct paths ever map to the same fragment id, silently overwriting one?

risk_map.reason: The per-file index must preserve changed-files order and represent renames, or the Review Route walks files in the wrong sequence.
challenge_questions: Is fragments.json order identical to changed-files.json?
Are rename/copy records carrying both old and new path?

risk_map.reason: Runner detection and the validator are new surfaces; under-testing them would let malformed analyses or wrong runner suggestions slip through.
challenge_questions: Is every required analysis section exercised by a malformed-input case?
Is the read-only guarantee of runner detection asserted, not just assumed?

file_walkthrough.explanation: New deep module: the Analysis Schema Validator. Validates structure, types, and the closed risk/omission vocabularies, returning located errors.
Adds file_fragment_id (traversal-safe hex id) and fragment_index_entry (one ordered index record, with omitted/reason for capped files).
Writes per-file fragments and fragments.json; rebuilds the fragments dir each run; disables core.quotePath for raw UTF-8 paths.
Read-only test-runner detection across Python/Node/Go/Rust/Make, returning a suggested command and the marker file that justifies it.

suspicious_omissions.summary: No per-file cap or exclusion is applied yet.
suspicious_omissions.detail: The index carries omitted/reason fields, but the Change Classifier that populates them is issue #7; large/excluded files are not yet omitted.

suspicious_omissions.summary: The cockpit's new sections are exercised end-to-end only by the lint tripwire, not a rendered-DOM assertion.
suspicious_omissions.detail: Section structure is validated via analysis.json; the HTML authoring itself relies on the post-write lint.

items: Run the per-file fragment table tests (multi-file order, rename, HTML-in-one-file, unusual path).
Run the analysis validator tables (a complete file passes; each malformed section reports a located error).
Run the runner-detection tables, including the read-only (no process-spawning import) assertion.

diagrams.title: Collection → analysis → cockpit
diagrams.source: graph LR; collect[collect_review_context] --> frags[per-file fragments]; frags --> analysis[analysis.json]; analysis --> validate[validate_analysis]; validate --> cockpit[review.html]
